text.title: ads-google-keywords Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ads-google-keywords. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Never use all broad match keywords without a comprehensive negative list. Broad match triggers on dozens of unrelated searches, wasting budget on irrelevant traffic.
rule.rationale: Broad match is designed for reach; it requires discipline with negatives to control waste.

rule.statement: Do not mix intents in a single ad group. Separate transactional keywords from informational keywords so ads can match the user's actual intent.
rule.rationale: Ad relevance is measured per keyword; mixing intents causes ads to be irrelevant to many triggered keywords, lowering CTR and Quality Score.

rule.statement: Keep 10-20 keywords per ad group, not 100+. Diluted keyword lists reduce relevance for each search term.
rule.rationale: Smaller ad groups with tight themes allow ads to match search intent more precisely.
